meta.source: Manual screenshot scrape · TradingView mobile (desktop offline)
meta.note: Pre-market Monday pull — US-listed cash equities (TLT, EWY, semis, financials) still reflect Friday Jul 3's close since the cash session hasn't opened. FX, rates, gold, VIX futures, and Asia equities are live: USDJPY broke above the 162.00 BOJ threshold overnight, USDKRW round-tripped back above the 1,530 Korea-gate line, and KOSPI gave back part of Friday's rebound.

liquidity.label: No new H.4.1 print yet — TGA/Reserves flat, SOFR eased 2bp below IORB
decomp: TGA holds at $880.2B, no new weekly print (next due Jul 9)
Reserves flat at $2.967T, no new weekly print
RRP flat at $2.18B — still historically low
SOFR eased to 3.64%, now 1bp below IORB (3.65%) — funding stress easing slightly
liquidity.trend_note: TGA +$38B/wk, reserves below $3T and falling — structural drain accelerating

credit.label: Stable — OAS tightened to 2.74%
credit.trend_note: HY OAS tightened from 2.83% to 2.74%, ALLY/CACC holding — credit conditions easing at the margin

inflation.label: Mixed — yields rising, oil flat
decomp: Real yield rising = headwind for growth
inflation.trend_note: Breakeven 2.24% anchored, oil flat near $69 — no new inflation impulse but 10Y/30Y rising suggests term premium, not inflation

vol.label: Low — VIX 15.84, MOVE 65.76
vol.trend_note: VIX 15.84, MOVE 65.76 — vol receding despite yield move, suggests equity-specific not macro fear

bias: Structural liquidity neutral — TGA drain ongoing but credit conditions have improved (HY OAS tightened to 2.74%). Short-term conditions improving. Long-term conditions still restrictive (10Y 4.485%, 30Y crossed 5%). The yield rise today is term premium expansion, not inflation reacceleration — breakevens anchored at 2.24%. Financials and value positioned better than long-duration growth in this environment. Korea gate 1/3 — USDKRW finally passed but KOSPI failed. Hold semis with tight sizing; no Korea adds.

counterargs.thesis: The weekend FX/Asia moves (USDJPY above 162, USDKRW above 1,530, KOSPI pullback) are a genuine regime shift, not just pre-market noise
supporting: HY OAS tightened to 2.74% — credit calm
counterargs.read: Today has two competing narratives: credit and FX are improving (HY OAS tighter, KRW stronger), but rates are moving against the duration thesis (10Y +4.5bps, 30Y crossing 5%). The Korea gate remains closed because KOSPI failed — FX condition finally opened for the first time but the market condition regressed. Net: cautiously constructive on semis, not yet actionable on Korea.

trade_table.rationale: DRAM +6.81%, CRDO +9.77%, SOXX +2.68%. Recovery from last week's selloff. EWY gate still closed but US semis intact. Conviction maintained.
10Y 4.485% and 30Y 5.003% moving wrong direction. Real yield rising. Duration thesis under pressure from rate move today.
Gate still closed 1/3. USDKRW finally passed (1,511 < 1,530) but KOSPI failed (7,656 < 8,200). EWY SMA20 not yet confirmed. Need all 3 conditions simultaneously.
ALLY/CACC/COF unrevised pre-market at Friday's stabilized levels. HY OAS steady at 2.75%.
USDJPY broke above 162.00 and DXY firmed +0.24% overnight — the dollar-weakness case needs a rethink if this holds into the session.
HYG and OAS unrevised pre-market at 2.75%. No fresh stress signal.
Jul-Sep reserve-drain window still ahead structurally; no new H.4.1 print to offset it this week.

catalysts.event: US cash equities open (today, pre-market pull)
catalysts.note: This pull reflects Friday's close for US names. Revisit once the 9:30am ET open prints live prices for EWY, semis, and financials.
Next scheduled TGA/Reserves print, plus 10Y auction bid-to-cover as a demand signal at these yields.
PCE-peak narrative confirmed or reversed. Hot print = duration selloff risk.
APH $165/$170 spread (APH $164.59) sits right at the lower strike. DRAM and EOSE spreads remain deep OTM.
Quarterly Refunding Announcement — auction sizes and TGA guidance for Q3.

plumbing.reserves_delta: flat at $2.967T — no new weekly print
plumbing.sofr_delta: ↓ eased 2bp to 3.64%, now 1bp below IORB (3.65%) — modest funding-stress relief

us10y.note: 4.485% and rising — wrong direction for duration thesis. Up from ~4.37% prior session.

us30y.note: Crossed 5.00% — term premium expanding. Long end pricing something beyond just policy.

us02y.note: 4.127% — policy rate expectations stable, not re-pricing sharply. Front end anchored.

spread_2s10s.note: 0.358% — curve steepening as long end moves more than front. Not inversion risk.

chain.note: Still marking Friday's -7.94% close. No new catalyst pre-market; watch for follow-through at the open.
Unrevised at Friday's -5.49% close. HBM thesis intact but momentum remains negative.
Still over 14% off the $274 ATH, unrevised since Friday's -6.63% close.
14 shares @ $189.66 — still above cost basis (+3.8%) but the cushion has shrunk sharply this week.
Off the $964 ATH by ~24%, unrevised since Friday's close.
50 shares @ $38.57 — still above cost basis (+8.6%), held up better than peers Friday.
Off the $96 ATH by ~27%. Watchlist only, no active position.
$165/$170 Jul 17 spread still sitting right at the lower strike — reassess once the cash session opens.
25 shares @ $84.21 — still down over 13% from cost basis.
$7.50/$10 Jul 17 spread (10 contracts). Thesis remains broken, still well below the floor. CLOSE PRIORITY.

vix.note: VIX 15.84 — still low. Rising slightly alongside yield move. Not a stress signal yet.

oil.note: Oil stable near $69. Hormuz reopening narrative holding. No new inflation impulse from energy.

gold.note: Gold flat at $4,164 near ATH. Real yield rising (2.245%) should headwind gold — resilience here is notable.

move.note: MOVE ticking up alongside 10Y/30Y yield rise. Still well below 100 stress threshold. Bond market not in distress.

duration_score.reason: 10Y rising (+9bps) while breakevens stable (+0.01) and credit tightening — yield rise is term premium / fiscal concern, not inflation reacceleration. Different from 'Fed easing' duration bull case. Negative for long-duration growth assets.

sector_rotation.current_regime: Mixed — Term Premium / Partial Liquidity Shock

liquidity_expansion.characteristics: Falling real rates, improving credit, weaker dollar
liquidity_expansion.match: Partial — credit improving, dollar soft, but real rates RISING

disinflationary_slowdown.characteristics: Falling long yields, sticky front end, slowing growth
disinflationary_slowdown.match: Partial — growth slowing signals but yields RISING not falling

recession.characteristics: Falling yields, widening credit spreads, falling earnings
recession.match: No — credit TIGHTENING, yields rising, no earnings collapse

inflation_reacceleration.characteristics: Rising yields, rising breakevens, stronger commodities
inflation_reacceleration.match: Partial — yields rising, but breakevens STABLE and oil flat

liquidity_shock.characteristics: Rising yields, widening credit spreads, dollar strength
liquidity_shock.match: Partial — yields rising but credit TIGHTENING (opposite of shock) and dollar flat

sector_rotation.confidence: Low — mixed signals prevent clean classification

actionable_output.structural_liquidity: Neutral — TGA drain ongoing but credit calm

actionable_output.macro_trend: Term Premium Expansion with Disinflationary undertones
actionable_output.likely_rotation: Financials / Value over long-duration growth near-term
actionable_output.next_catalyst: 10Y Auction Jul 9 — bid-to-cover will clarify term premium thesis

confidence_meta.reason: 1 catalyst(s) within 48h (US cash equities open (today, pre-market pull)) — regime call has elevated uncertainty until results are known

structured_conclusion.macro_trend: Term Premium Expansion / Partial Disinflationary Slowdown
